![][image1]  
You can select your VM here. The common ones are E2Micro, E2Small, E2Medium, and N2 Standard. I’ve chosen E2 Micro which is the smallest of them all as this is just demonstration

In the OS section you can choose your OS, the default is Debian, and I’ve chosen that:  
![][image2]

In the Network section allow HTTP and HTTPS this helps discover your Ext IP as an HTTP URL  
![][image3]

It may take a while after you hit create, GCP will load your instance for you  
![][image4]  
Click on the SSH button, it’ll connect you to your VM (it’ll open an authorise page click authorize and proceed)  
![][image5]  
I’ll deploy this very simple Flask CRUD app I have running locally in my machine:  
![][image6]  
As you can see it doesn’t only need the code files, but also the static/image files for the bg. 

Once the VM is open, install the necessary dependencies for your project since mine is python based I am using: `sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y sudo apt install python3-pip python3-venv git -y`

Now I am getting a webpage with functional layout but not the image. This is expected as I haven’t uploaded the image.   
![][image7]  
Now after uploading, adjusting paths, and making some minor changes for the end product to look good, we have our complete webpage as an accessible link\! The image confirms this:  
![][image8]  
To confirm cross browser compatibility I have opened the same link on Safari too:  
![][image9]  
I’ve successfully hosted a python flask website on GCP E2Micro\!
